result:
Done. The spec is fully implemented and verified — the project builds cleanly and all behavioral checks pass, using 2 of the 10 build cycles.

**What I did** in `src/CsvRow.calr`, following the existing fixture's conventions (inline signatures, sequential `f00N` IDs, explicit effect declarations):

- **`EscapeField`** (`f002`) — delegates to the existing `NeedsQuoting`; when quoting is needed it doubles internal quotes with `(replace field "\"" "\"\"")` and wraps the result in double-quotes, otherwise returns the field verbatim. Pure (`§E{}`).
- **`JoinRow`** (`f003`) — escapes each field and joins with commas via a first-element-flag accumulator loop; an empty list falls through to the empty string. Pure (`§E{}`).
- **`SplitRow`** (`f004`) — an index-based scanner: a field starting with `"` is parsed to the next un-doubled quote (doubled quotes contribute one literal quote, commas/CR/LF inside are content); otherwise the field runs to the next comma or end of line. A comma after a field continues the loop, so a trailing comma yields a final empty field, and the empty line yields a single empty field. It declares `§E{mut}` because this project pins `CalorEnforceEffects=true` and the compiler's effect analyzer requires `mut` for `§PUSH` (local list building) — the function is still externally pure, per the spec's constraint.

Two things I learned from the Calor test corpus that mattered: an *empty* `§LIST` needs an explicit `§/LIST{id}` closer (the indent-only rule alone fails to parse it — that was my one build error), and local variable assignment is effect-free while collection pushes are not.

**Verification**: beyond the clean `dotnet build`, I inspected the generated C# and ran a throwaway console harness (outside the project, per the no-test-files rule) against the built `Src.dll`: unit checks for all `EscapeField` quoting cases, `JoinRow` edge cases (empty list, all-empty fields), `SplitRow("")` → `[""]`, plus a brute-force `SplitRow(JoinRow(x)) == x` sweep over every 1–3 field row drawn from a tricky alphabet (`""`, `","`, `"\""`, `"\"\""`, `"a\"b"`, `"\r\n"`, `",\""`) — 584 round-trips, all passing.
